title: How interest rates reshape farm management decisions this year
content: Ag Marketing IQ In Depth: Ag economist David Widmar discusses how the cost of borrowing money impacts farm expansion and grain storage decisions. One tip: Fed action won’t solve the problem.

title: Bayer earnings top estimates on crop science, soybean gains
content: Q1 adjusted EBITDA beats forecast by 14%. Soybean sales doubled on licensing payment from seed tech dispute and dicamba re-approval aids prices.

title: Maryland farmer puts new technology to test
content: Field Snapshot: As input costs surge, Chris Weaver manages his 1,000 acres with precision tech and 91-year-old wisdom.

title: Pest outlook 2026: What’s coming and what to watch now
content: Wisconsin farmers face heightened pest threats this season. Stay ahead of these three early-season pests with scouting tips and timing strategies that can protect yield before damage takes hold in corn, soybean and alfalfa fields.

title: How does inflation impact land values?
content: Land Sales: Farmland holds on to value and appreciation for investors — and farmers.

title: Farm to fork: An inside look at pork processing
content: Pork Pulse: Minnesota’s emerging pork industry leaders visit Hormel Foods’ flagship facilities, exploring the complete journey from producer to consumer.

title: Nutrition is vital for heifer conception rates
content: Hit pause before turning your bred heifers out to pasture. Ensure they have enough nutrients and don’t “starve” on lush grass.

title: Rice, soybean irrigation costs vary widely across eastern Arkansas
content: University of Arkansas research maps the true cost of pumping groundwater across the state, revealing dramatic differences driven by water depth and crop type.

title: How to make more money by growing low-carbon crops
content: Discover this and more at the TopSoil Summit, slated for June 10 at the Riverside Casino & Golf Resort in Riverside, Iowa.

title: How one tick bite sparked a new farm-to-table business model
content: A Missouri couple turned personal health struggles into The Safe Spoon, a restaurant powered by local farmers and allergen-safe food.
